title: Parity Anomaly of a Single Two Plus One Dimensional Dirac Fermion
summary: A single 2+1-dimensional Dirac fermion cannot be regulated so as to preserve both large-gauge invariance and parity; choosing gauge invariance leaves a parity-odd Chern-Simons term whose coefficient is half-integer modulo integers.
assumptions: A single charged Dirac fermion in 2+1 dimensions is coupled to a background U(1) gauge field.
The low-energy theory is considered modulo integer counterterms in the Chern-Simons level.
regime: Continuum odd-dimensional fermion effective action with large-gauge transformations included.
scope: Background concept needed to interpret the half-level Chern-Simons response of a massive Dirac cone.

source_anchors.notes: Redlich states the gauge-invariance versus parity tradeoff explicitly.
Niemi-Semenoff tie odd-dimensional effective actions to anomaly data.
Witten uses the parity-anomaly logic as the physical explanation for the half-level term.

math_expressions.context: The parity-odd part of the regulated effective action is only defined modulo integer Chern-Simons counterterms.

symbols.meaning: parity-odd part of the effective action after integrating out the Dirac fermion
integer counterterm ambiguity allowed by large-gauge invariance

failure_modes: On a lattice or in a doubled continuum theory the half-integer contribution can combine with other cones to give an integer total level.
retrieval_hints: Use when explaining why a single Dirac cone produces a half-level term but a complete microscopic theory must have integer total level.
